lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY + heavy (NOT yet runtime-proven). Closes all 3 cloud-ml writes: POST /v1/cloud-ml (createcloudml), PUT /v1/cloud-ml/{cloud_ml_id} (updatecloudml), DELETE /v1/cloud-ml/{cloud_ml_id} (deletecloudml). A cloud-ml resource is a billable, GPU-capable ML serving stack provisioned onto a SKE cluster, so this lifecycle NEVER actually provisions one. Pattern: soft-capture cluster_id/image_id from the read-only images/list, soft-capture cloud_ml_id from the list against a DELIBERATELY-NONEXISTENT field so the {cloud_ml_id} placeholder stays LITERAL -> guaranteed 404, then CALL each write once (optional+grouped+broad expect_status) to record coverage without touching a real resource. Marked heavy:true (gated behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY) as defense in depth. POST body is the data/api_bodies.json createcloudml shape (cloud_ml_name/cluster_id/image_id/product_group_id + analytics/application/copilot resource sizing); PUT body is the api_bodies updatecloudml shape ({description}). The doc-sample ids in createcloudml are replaced with soft-captured/{unique} values where possible; expected 400/422 (needs real cluster_id/image_id/product_group_id) or 403. probe-reads exercises the id-bound GETs (getcloudml + clusterproduct/estimate/check-releasable) when a real id is present.

steps.name: list-cloud-ml
steps._note: cloud_ml_id captured against a nonexistent field so the placeholder stays literal -> PUT/DELETE/GET-by-id hit a guaranteed 404 (records coverage, touches no real resource). cluster_id soft-captured if a resource exists.

steps.name: create-cloud-ml

json.description: API regression suite — coverage-only, never provisioned

steps._note: BILLABLE ML serving stack — never actually provisioned. Body from data/api_bodies.json createcloudml (UNPROVEN) with the doc-sample ids replaced by soft-captured cluster_id/image_id (product_group_id omitted — no capture source, the create is expected to 4xx anyway); expected 400/422 (needs real cluster_id/image_id/product_group_id) or 403. Deliberately NOT chained to a delete of a real resource.

steps.name: update-cloud-ml
steps._note: In-place update (updatecloudml). Body {description} from data/api_bodies.json updatecloudml. Targets the literal {cloud_ml_id} -> 404.

steps.name: delete-cloud-ml
steps._note: destructive — only runs under SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Targets the literal {cloud_ml_id} (no real resource) -> expected 404. optional+broad so it records coverage without deleting a real resource.
